description_long: This sculpture features an abstract composition of geometric and organic forms, rising vertically from a rectangular pedestal. The base is solid and blocky, supporting a dynamic arrangement of sharp triangular shapes interwoven with rounded, donut-like rings. The interplay between the angular and curved elements creates a sense of upward movement and tension. The surface appears to be made of a uniform, matte stone or concrete material, with subtle textural variations. The overall color is a muted grayish-brown, giving the piece a monolithic and modernist aesthetic. There are no visible inscriptions, plaques, or artist signatures on the pedestal or sculpture.
description: An abstract sculpture of interlocking geometric and organic forms in matte grayish-brown stone, mounted on a rectangular pedestal.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full arrangement of angular and rounded forms, with the triangular shapes and rings most prominent.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the sculpture's narrow profile and the stacked, layered nature of the rounded elements.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the geometric and organic forms, mirroring the front but with less detail visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the depth and layering of the rounded forms, with the angular elements receding.
attribute_location_context: No specific environmental context is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
